template.title: Github Fronting Starter
template.summary: Template-suggested Github API/MCP fronting structure with reviewed capability candidates.
template.description: Starts a governed Github fronting project from reviewed starter data. Verify backend operations, auth posture, and policy boundaries against the real integration before locking.
template.recommendedBrief: Create a governed ANIP fronting layer for Github. Expose selected behavior-shaped capabilities instead of raw backend operations. Read operations must preserve actor-visible scope. Write-adjacent operations must prepare previews or require approval before downstream mutation.

documents.title: Github governed fronting intent
documents.content:
# Github Governed Fronting Intent

> Imported from starter template `github-fronting-starter`. Treat this as draft guidance, not discovered truth.

Expose a governed Github capability surface in front of native API or MCP-backed operations.

## Template-suggested capabilities
- github.repo.search_context: Search bounded GitHub repository issues and pull requests without exposing raw repository tools.
- github.issue.prepare: Prepare a GitHub issue preview with labels and assignees before any issue is created.
- github.pr.comment.prepare: Prepare a pull request comment from review or release context without posting it.
- github.workflow.dispatch.request: Prepare a governed GitHub Actions workflow dispatch request and stop for approval.
- github.release_notes.prepare: Prepare bounded release notes from selected issues or pull requests without creating a GitHub release.

## Governance posture
- Raw Github tools are not exposed directly to agents.
- Reads return bounded actor-visible context.
- Write-adjacent actions prepare previews or require explicit approval before execution.
- Missing required inputs trigger clarification instead of guessing.

documents.title: Github integration evidence
documents.content:
# Github Integration Evidence Template

> Imported from starter template `github-fronting-starter`. Verify every operation against your chosen native API/MCP source before locking.

Native API connection: GITHUB_API_URL
MCP connection: GITHUB_MCP_ENDPOINT

## Template-suggested backend operations
- github.api.search_repository_context: GET /github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/context/search
- github.api.prepare_issue: POST /github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/preview
- github.api.prepare_pull_request_comment: POST /github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/comments/preview
- github.api.request_workflow_dispatch: POST /github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{workflow_id}/dispatches/preview
- github.api.prepare_release_notes: GET /github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/release-notes/preview

## Secret posture
- Studio stores secret references only; project exports must not contain tokens.
- Teams can implement either native API or MCP backend seams behind the same governed ANIP contract.

documents.title: Github developer evidence
documents.content:
# Github Developer Evidence

Imported from starter template `github-fronting-starter`. This is developer-owned evidence for Studio Autopilot and generated-service review.

This document turns starter operations into explicit ANIP runtime governance and input-contract evidence. Review it against the real integration before publishing.

## Capability Evidence Summary

| capability_id | service_id | operation_type | side_effect_level | inputs | backend_operation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | ---: | --- |
| github.repo.search_context | github-fronting | read | read | 5 | github.api.search_repository_context |
| github.issue.prepare | github-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 7 | github.api.prepare_issue |
| github.pr.comment.prepare | github-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 6 | github.api.prepare_pull_request_comment |
| github.workflow.dispatch.request | github-governance | write | write_adjacent | 6 | github.api.request_workflow_dispatch |
| github.release_notes.prepare | github-fronting | read | read | 5 | github.api.prepare_release_notes |

## Reviewed Developer Evidence

documents.title: Github governed fronting intent
documents.content:
# Github Governed Fronting Intent

> Imported from starter template `github-fronting-starter`. Treat this as draft guidance, not discovered truth.

Expose a governed Github capability surface in front of native API or MCP-backed operations.

## Template-suggested capabilities
- github.repo.search_context: Search bounded GitHub repository issues and pull requests without exposing raw repository tools.
- github.issue.prepare: Prepare a GitHub issue preview with labels and assignees before any issue is created.
- github.pr.comment.prepare: Prepare a pull request comment from review or release context without posting it.
- github.workflow.dispatch.request: Prepare a governed GitHub Actions workflow dispatch request and stop for approval.
- github.release_notes.prepare: Prepare bounded release notes from selected issues or pull requests without creating a GitHub release.

## Governance posture
- Raw Github tools are not exposed directly to agents.
- Reads return bounded actor-visible context.
- Write-adjacent actions prepare previews or require explicit approval before execution.
- Missing required inputs trigger clarification instead of guessing.
